parts.label: a
parts.markScheme: B1 Correct answer 23/20 oe. Accept: 1 3/20. Do not accept: 5/9.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Write 0.375 as a fraction in its simplest form.
parts.markScheme: B1 3/8. Accept: any equivalent fraction simplified to 3/8. Do not accept: 375/1000 unless simplified.

parts.label: a
parts.markScheme: M1 Combine coefficients 4 + 7 - 3. A1 8a. Accept: equivalent algebraic form 8a. Do not accept: 8.

questions.context: A drink is made by mixing orange juice and water in the ratio 3:5.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: If 24 ml of orange juice is used, work out the total amount of drink made.
parts.markScheme: M1 3 parts correspond to 24 ml. M1 One part = 24 ÷ 3 = 8 ml. A1 Total parts = 8 so total = 8 × 8 = 64 ml.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Work out the value of x.
parts.markScheme: M1 Angles on a straight line sum to 180°. M1 180 − 70. A1 x = 110°.

questions.contextSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 220"><polygon points="80,170 220,170 80,70" fill="none" stroke="oklch(0.85 0 0)"/><rect x="80" y="158" width="12" height="12" fill="none" stroke="oklch(0.85 0 0)"/><text x="130" y="185" fill="oklch(0.72 0.18 250)" font-size="13">8</text><text x="85" y="110" fill="oklch(0.85 0 0)" font-size="13">30°</text></svg>

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Find the length of the hypotenuse.
parts.markScheme: M1 Use cos(theta) = adjacent / hypotenuse. M1 cos(30) = 8 / h. M1 h = 8 / cos(30). A1 Exact answer 16/sqrt(3) or rationalised 8sqrt(3)/3 oe.

questions.context: The table shows the number of books read by students in a class.
questions.contextSvg: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 220"><text x="40" y="60" fill="oklch(0.85 0 0)" font-size="13">Books: 1 2 3 4</text><text x="40" y="90" fill="oklch(0.85 0 0)" font-size="13">Frequency: 3 5 4 2</text></svg>

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Calculate the mean number of books read.
parts.markScheme: M1 Multiply values by frequencies. M1 1x3 + 2x5 + 3x4 + 4x2. M1 Sum = 33. M1 Total frequency = 14. A1 Mean = 33/14.

parts.label: a
parts.prompt: Vector AB = a and BC = b. Find AC in terms of a and b.
parts.markScheme: B1 Use triangle law of vectors. M1 AC = AB + BC. M1 Substitute vectors. A1 AC = a + b. Accept: vector addition statement. Do not accept: a - b.
